character.role.semantic_definition: The character's formal title, rank, or institutional affiliation within the story's world. Specifies how the world categorizes their authority or function.
character.role.example: Senior Claims Magistrate, Helios Prize Court
character.role.anti_example: A brilliant legal mind who has spent decades navigating interplanetary bureaucracies
character.role.anti_example_reason: Describes psychology/backstory instead of a formal position.

character.function_in_narrative.semantic_definition: Explains what this character does for the story's argument — their mechanical/thematic function relative to the protagonist and meta thesis.
character.function_in_narrative.example: The intellectual spine who sees Morrow most clearly because their profession is examining the gap between law and practice.
character.function_in_narrative.anti_example: Provides important legal context for the story.
character.function_in_narrative.anti_example_reason: Too vague; does not describe a structural function.

character.psychology.semantic_definition: Defining psychological fact about the character — a behavioral observation showing how they operate and why.
character.psychology.example: Caine maintains the system because the alternative is worse; equal in talent to Morrow but oriented toward preservation over reinvention.
character.psychology.anti_example: Intelligent, analytical, somewhat cold but fair.
character.psychology.anti_example_reason: Trait list rather than a behavioral observation.

theme.definition.semantic_definition: Universal statement of the theme in abstract terms, independent of the story's specifics.
theme.definition.example: Institutions calcify when they confuse procedural adherence with moral legitimacy.
theme.definition.anti_example_reason: Describes a setting detail instead of an abstract theme.

theme.manifestation.semantic_definition: Describes how the theme shows up in this story's people, institutions, and events.
theme.manifestation.example: Embodied by Caine's loyalty to legal instruments contrasted with Morrow's willingness to weaponize them.
theme.manifestation.anti_example_reason: Non-specific; lacks anchors to story elements.

faction.structural_role.semantic_definition: Explains which structural position the faction occupies in the narrative argument (e.g., system preservers, opportunists).
faction.structural_role.example: Acts as the legal immune system that prevents charismatic actors from rewriting precedent overnight.

faction.character.semantic_definition: Describes how the faction behaves — its values, temperament, and decision patterns.
faction.character.example: Cold, procedural, and allergic to improvisation; treats every problem as an audit trail to be reconciled.

arc.act.semantic_definition: Describes the protagonist's psychological and situational state during the act — beliefs, assets, blind spots.
arc.act.example: Act Two: intoxicated by new leverage, convinced the system can be bent indefinitely; euphoric but blind to collateral damage.

protagonist.core_trait.semantic_definition: Single defining psychological fact from which all other behavior flows.
protagonist.core_trait.example: Believes systems exist to be bent into alignment with their personal thesis.

protagonist.the_flaw.semantic_definition: Explains why the protagonist cannot coexist with the world as-is — the inevitability of their downfall/transformation.
protagonist.the_flaw.example: Cannot distinguish between necessary structural stress and wanton destruction of collective trust.

protagonist.the_hunger.semantic_definition: Names the appetite pushing the protagonist beyond rational self-interest.
protagonist.the_hunger.example: Needs every institution to testify that his thesis is correct, even if it means breaking them to extract a confession.

protagonist.core_belief.statement.semantic_definition: How the protagonist would justify their existence in their own voice.
protagonist.core_belief.statement.example: I fix what lesser minds break, even if I have to break them to do it.

relationship.dynamic.semantic_definition: Explains how the relationship feels moment-to-moment — the push/pull dynamic.
relationship.dynamic.example: Mutual admiration poisoned by incompatible definitions of loyalty.

relationship.dramatic_function.semantic_definition: What this relationship reveals about the meta thesis or protagonist.
relationship.dramatic_function.example: Shows how institutional loyalty collapses when personal myth-making becomes contagious.

set_piece.thematic_argument.semantic_definition: States the theme this set piece demonstrates, tying spectacle to thesis.
set_piece.thematic_argument.example: Confirms that Morrow can win any confrontation except the one with collective belief.
